# PULSEWIRE

> PULSEWIRE is a cyberpunk arcade thriller for iPhone — a minimalist neon-noir game. You trace one living wire through a swarm of hunting patrols to its sink. Safe play locks you out; the only points worth having are stolen by skimming death — graze a patrol by a hair for a NEAR-DEATH +1000 and time fractures into slow motion.

PULSEWIRE is a free download on the Apple App Store (iOS 17.0 or later): https://apps.apple.com/us/app/pulsewire/id6778034497

It is a single-player, one-finger game made by Divera Game Studio. 100% offline: no ads, no accounts, no in-app data collection. One optional in-app unlock (the System Studio) adds a level editor, an AI arena, and director/replay tools.

## What kind of game it is

A cyberpunk arcade thriller that blends several genres: it plays like a casual arcade game (instant, one-finger, pick-up-and-play), carries the spatial pressure of a puzzle game, the reflexes of an action game, and a retro neon aesthetic. Above all it is a nerve/tension game — you are rewarded for grazing death, not avoiding it.

Keywords: cyberpunk game, iOS game, iPhone game, arcade game, action game, one-finger game, offline game, no ads, indie game, thriller, nerve, tension.

## Core mechanics

- **Trace, don't tap**: hold and drag one finger to guide a wire from a source to a sink.
- **Skim to score**: the closer you graze a hostile patrol without touching it, the higher you score. A hair's-breadth pass is a NEAR-DEATH +1000.
- **Rewind, don't die**: lift to drop an anchor; if you get clipped you rewind to it instead of dying — taxed on every use.
- **Core Breach**: thread an atom core for +2000.
- **21 levels**: difficulty ramps from clean lanes to SYSTEM CRASH, with a dozen hostile enemy classes.
- **The System Studio** (one-time in-app unlock): AI Arena, a full level editor, and a Director's Cut replay/export tool.
